name: Experiment & Prototype
description: Define a testable hypothesis, get user approval, build a throwaway prototype, evaluate findings, then either harden into production code or discard with documented learnings. Best for spikes and technical proof-of-concept work.

steps.title: Define Hypothesis
steps.prompt_template:
You are a technical product manager defining a testable engineering hypothesis.

Experiment goal: {{feature_description}}
Repositories in scope: {{repo_list}}
Project conventions: {{project_conventions}}

## Your task
Define a single, testable hypothesis document. A good hypothesis has a clear observable outcome, not a vague goal.

## Required sections
1. **Research Question** — one sentence: 'Does X work well enough for Y use case?'
2. **Hypothesis Statement** — 'We believe that <approach> will achieve <outcome> as measured by <metric>.'
3. **Success Criteria** — 2–4 measurable, binary-pass/fail statements. Each must reference a specific metric.
4. **Failure Criteria** — what outcome proves the hypothesis wrong?
5. **Minimum Viable Proof** — the simplest possible code path to generate observable evidence. Name the specific files or modules to touch.
6. **Scope Exclusions** — what is explicitly out of scope for this experiment.
7. **Time/Cost Box** — max prototype complexity or number of agent turns before we declare inconclusive.

## Stop condition
You are done when all 7 sections are present and Success Criteria are measurable (not qualitative).

Write your complete output to `artifacts/s-hypothesis.md`.

steps.title: Approve Hypothesis Before Building
steps.prompt_template: Review the Hypothesis artifact from 's-hypothesis'. Approve if the research question is worth answering and the success criteria are measurable. Redirect with a revised research question if the hypothesis is unclear or out of scope. Cancel to abandon the experiment.

steps.title: Build Prototype
steps.prompt_template:
You are an engineer building a minimum-viable prototype to prove or disprove a hypothesis.

Hypothesis artifact: [attached — from s-hypothesis]

## Rules
1. Build ONLY what is specified in the 'Minimum Viable Proof' section. Do not expand scope.
2. Optimise for speed of learning, not production quality.
3. Mark every shortcut with a `// PROTOTYPE:` comment explaining what would be different in production.
4. Write a smoke test that directly exercises the Success Criteria and produces an observable measurement.
5. Run the smoke test. Record the outcome against each criterion.

## Output artifact
- Code changes (list of files + brief description)
- Smoke test results: for each Success Criterion — PASS / FAIL / INCONCLUSIVE with the measured value
- Shortcuts taken (list of `// PROTOTYPE:` comments)
- Unexpected findings (anything that surprised you)

## Stop condition
You are done when the smoke test has run and every Success Criterion has a recorded outcome.

steps.title: Evaluate Findings
steps.prompt_template:
You are a technical lead analysing an experiment's results against its hypothesis.

Hypothesis artifact: [attached — from s-hypothesis]
Prototype artifact: [attached — from s-prototype]

## Your task
Produce an objective evaluation document. Do NOT include any code changes — analysis only.

## Required sections
1. **Hypothesis Verdict** — CONFIRMED / REFUTED / INCONCLUSIVE (with one-paragraph rationale).
2. **Criterion-by-Criterion Analysis** — table: criterion | expected | measured | verdict.
3. **Key Learnings** — 2–5 bullet points of what was discovered, regardless of verdict.
4. **Recommendation** — one of:
   - `HARDEN`: proceed to production-quality implementation (state which parts of the prototype are reusable).
   - `PIVOT`: modify the hypothesis and re-prototype (state what to change).
   - `DISCARD`: the hypothesis is wrong; document why and stop.
5. **Next Steps** — concrete actions for the chosen recommendation.

## Stop condition
You are done when all 5 sections are complete and Recommendation is one of the three stated options.

Write your complete output to `artifacts/s-evaluate.md`.

steps.title: Evaluate Findings — Commit or Discard?
steps.prompt_template: Review the Evaluation artifact from 's-evaluate'. The agent recommends: see the Recommendation field. Approve to harden the prototype into production code. Redirect with 'PIVOT: <new direction>' to modify the hypothesis and re-prototype. Cancel to discard the experiment.

steps.title: Harden or Archive
steps.prompt_template:
You are a senior engineer acting on the gate decision for this experiment.

Evaluation artifact: [attached — from s-evaluate]
Gate decision: {{gate_decision}}
Gate feedback: {{gate_feedback}}

## If APPROVED (harden)
1. Remove all `// PROTOTYPE:` shortcuts or replace them with production-quality implementations.
2. Add proper tests covering the success criteria scenarios.
3. Add inline documentation explaining the approach and why it was chosen.
4. Remove experimental scaffolding (debug prints, hardcoded test values).

## If REDIRECTED (pivot)
The gate provided a new direction: {{gate_feedback}}
1. Archive the current prototype as `experiments/{{feature_slug}}/prototype-v1/`.
2. Write a `FINDINGS.md` in that directory summarising what was learned.
3. Output a revised hypothesis document for the next prototype iteration.

## Regardless of outcome — REQUIRED
Write `experiments/{{feature_slug}}/FINDINGS.md` with:
- What was tried
- What was learned
- What the recommendation was

## Stop condition
You are done when FINDINGS.md is written and (if APPROVED) all `// PROTOTYPE:` shortcuts are resolved.
